newsSince: Seagate confirmed nearline HDD capacity is essentially sold out/allocated through calendar 2027 as Mozaic 4+ HAMR scales to 44TB drives, with management citing AI-driven data-gravity demand. Recent quarter delivered record gross margins and ~44% revenue growth, with Q4 FY26 non-GAAP EPS guidance ~$5.00 (well above consensus). Multiple PT raises (JPMorgan $920, BofA $1,000, street-high ~$1,150). Strongly positive but heavily discovered (+21% since June 9).

sources.label: Seagate sold out through 2027 as AI reshapes HDD demand (Yahoo/Trefis)
Seagate CEO on AI data demand; BofA raises PT (TheStreet)
STX valuation after Mozaic 4+ HAMR rollout (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck master list, storage & AI data-platforms bucket. Capacity sold out through 2027, 44TB HAMR scaling, record margins and an above-consensus Q4 guide are clear fundamental positives; but hidden alpha is exhausted and the name is heavily covered, so a positive score drift without a peer re-rank fits the lens.
verdicts.bull: Nearline allocated through CY2027; Mozaic 4+ HAMR at 44TB; record margins; Q4 EPS guide ~$5 beats consensus.
verdicts.neutral: Fundamentals excellent but fully discovered; bottleneck no longer under-owned.
verdicts.bear: HDD cyclicality, demand pull-forward risk, top-pick crowding and insider selling.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: HAMR capacity sold out through 2027 on AI data-gravity demand.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical reversal / demand pull-forward once the upcycle peaks.
